name: Llama-3.3-Nemotron-Super-49B v1 (Reasoning On)
license: NVIDIA Open Model License + Llama 3.3 Community License Agreement
archDisplay: Dense Transformer, NAS-compressed from Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct (variable FFN + skip-attention blocks)
note: Toggleable reasoning mode via system prompt; NAS-compressed to fit a single H100/H200 at high throughput.
tpsNote: ~30GB at Q4 exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB; no verified 4090 figure found.

results.notes: LiveCodeBench (2408-2502 window), Reasoning On, Final checkpoint. From Table 4 of the technical report; this benchmark is absent from the HF model card entirely for this model.

IFEval, Reasoning On. From Table 4 of the technical report, which reports both modes for the final LN-Super checkpoint (on=89.2, off=89.0). The HF model card/NIM docs publish only a single IFEval value (89.21) explicitly labeled 'Reasoning Off' with no Reasoning-On figure at all -- so this paper-sourced on-mode value fills a genuine gap versus the card, though note the close-but-not-identical off-mode figures (89.0 in the paper vs 89.21 on the card) suggest some methodological variance between the two official publications.

BFCL V2 Live, Reasoning On. From Table 4 of the technical report, which splits the final LN-Super checkpoint into on=73.7 / off=73.9. CAVEAT: the HF model card/NIM docs publish this exact same number (73.7) but label it 'Reasoning Off' (with no separate on-value given there) -- i.e. the two official NVIDIA sources disagree on which reasoning mode 73.7 belongs to. Since the paper's own off-mode figure for this row is 73.9 (not 73.7), the numeric evidence favors the paper's on-mode attribution, but this is a real conflict between NVIDIA's own official docs worth flagging to a human reviewer rather than silently resolving.

Arena-Hard, Reasoning On. From Table 4 of the technical report: for the final LN-Super checkpoint the row reads 'on=88.3 | off=—' (no off-mode figure reported). CAVEAT: the HF model card/NIM docs publish this identical number (88.3) but label it 'Reasoning Off' instead, and that exact value/label pairing is already recorded in the sibling 'Reasoning Off' corpus entry for this model. This is a direct labeling conflict between NVIDIA's two official sources for the identical figure -- flagging for reviewer judgment rather than fabricating a resolution. Section 6.2 of the paper's body text corroborates an Arena-Hard score in the high-80s for the RLHF'd LN-Super (RPO raised it from 69.1 to 88.1), which is more consistent with this being the model's general/default (would suggest either mode) chat-preference score than a mode-specific figure.

Humanity's Last Exam, independently measured by Artificial Analysis on the 'Reasoning' (Reasoning On) variant of this model. HLE was not previously tracked for this model under either mode. Verified by manually aligning the model-name list against the score list on the AA model page (position 21 of 26, value 7%).

IFBench, independently measured by Artificial Analysis on the 'Reasoning' (Reasoning On) variant — distinct benchmark from the already-tracked IFEval. Verified by manually aligning the model-name list against the score list on the AA model page (position 22 of 24, value 38%).

verificationNotes: Confirmed via HF model card (fetched): same model card as Reasoning-Off variant; all four Reasoning-On benchmark scores match exactly (MATH500 96.6, AIME25 58.4, GPQA 66.67, MBPP 91.3).
